purpose: Atoms are the smallest reusable UI components that cannot be broken down further

decisions.title: Single file component
decisions.rationale: Atoms should be self-contained in a single file for easy reuse
decisions.alternative: Consider splitting if the component grows complex

decisions.title: No internal state
decisions.rationale: Atoms should be stateless - use molecules for components with state
decisions.alternative: Use useState or useReducer in molecules instead

decisions.title: Named exports
decisions.rationale: Use named exports for better tree-shaking
decisions.alternative: Default exports work but are less explicit

tree.root: Is this the smallest possible unit?

branches.outcome: This is an Atom — place in templates/atoms/
Break it down: extract sub-pieces as Atoms, compose in a Molecule

changelog.change: Initial atom tier definition with drizzle, zod, form, query atoms

purpose: Molecules are composite components that combine atoms with local state

decisions.title: Props drilling avoidance
decisions.rationale: Molecules manage their own state to avoid prop drilling
decisions.alternative: For deeply nested state, use context in organisms

decisions.title: Composition over creation
decisions.rationale: Molecules compose atoms rather than creating new UI
decisions.alternative: Reuse existing atoms before creating new ones

branches.outcome: This is a Molecule — place in templates/molecules/
Consider making it a parameterised Atom
Promote to Feature or use context

changelog.change: Initial molecules: drizzle table_body, zod schema_block, server_fn handler, query hooks, form component, data table, auth config

purpose: Layouts define the structure and wrapping of routes using Jinja2 extends/block

decisions.title: File-based routing
decisions.rationale: Layouts map directly to route file structure
decisions.alternative: Use nested routes for complex hierarchies

decisions.title: Import hoisting via render_imports()
decisions.rationale: Layouts drain the ImportCollector as the first statement so imports appear at the top of every generated file
decisions.alternative: Without hoisting, imports would be scattered where atoms render them

decisions.title: Shared state via context
decisions.rationale: Layouts can provide context to child routes
decisions.alternative: Keep layout state minimal to avoid coupling

changelog.change: Initial layouts: base, component, route — all with render_imports() drain

purpose: Features are complete CRUD modules with routes, components, and server functions

decisions.title: Convention over configuration
decisions.rationale: Features follow strict conventions for consistency
decisions.alternative: Custom structures require more maintenance

decisions.title: Server functions colocated
decisions.rationale: Server functions live next to their route for co-location
decisions.alternative: Extract to lib/ only if shared across features

decisions.title: Barrel exports
decisions.rationale: Features export everything from index for clean imports
decisions.alternative: Barrels can hide internal structure - use with care

branches.outcome: Use tsx generate feature — creates 7+ files
Use tsx generate feature with operations=[list]
Pass auth=true to generate feature or add it with tsx add auth-guard

changelog.change: Initial feature: schema + server_fn + query + table + form + 2 route pages

purpose: Database schemas define table structures using Drizzle ORM with full TypeScript type inference

decisions.title: Type safety
decisions.rationale: Schemas generate TypeScript types automatically via Drizzle inferSelect/inferInsert
decisions.alternative: Manual types can drift from schema

decisions.title: Idiomatic column order
decisions.rationale: id (auto-increment), created_at, updated_at, then domain columns
decisions.alternative: Consistent order helps readability and tooling

decisions.title: SQLite by default
decisions.rationale: TSX targets SQLite for zero-config local development
decisions.alternative: PostgreSQL dialect is a drop-in swap in drizzle.config.ts

branches.outcome: Use id field type with references

changelog.change: Initial schema template: all 11 FieldTypes, timestamps, soft_delete, relations

purpose: Server functions are type-safe RPC calls between TanStack Start client and server

decisions.title: Named exports
decisions.rationale: Server functions must be named exports for tree-shaking
decisions.alternative: Anonymous functions lose type information

decisions.title: Input validation via Zod
decisions.rationale: Always validate inputs to prevent injection and type errors
decisions.alternative: Skip only for trusted internal callers

decisions.title: createServerFn pattern
decisions.rationale: Uses @tanstack/start createServerFn() for automatic serialisation
decisions.alternative: Raw API routes lack type safety

branches.outcome: Takes validated input, returns created row
Takes id + partial input, returns updated row
Pass auth=true — wraps handler with session check

changelog.change: Initial server_fn template: all 4 CRUD operations with optional auth guard

purpose: TanStack Query hooks provide caching, background refetch, and Suspense-compatible data fetching

decisions.title: Query keys as arrays
decisions.rationale: Use [resource, id] format for fine-grained cache invalidation
decisions.alternative: String keys prevent partial invalidation

decisions.title: useSuspenseQuery by default
decisions.rationale: Suspense mode integrates cleanly with TanStack Router loaders
decisions.alternative: useQuery with isLoading checks is more verbose

decisions.title: Mutation returns invalidateQueries
decisions.rationale: After a mutation, invalidate the relevant list query key
decisions.alternative: Optimistic updates are faster but more complex

branches.outcome: Add page param to queryKey and queryFn

changelog.change: Initial query template: useSuspenseQuery + useMutation with queryKey atoms

purpose: TanStack Form components provide type-safe, field-level validation with minimal re-renders

decisions.title: Controlled inputs
decisions.rationale: Forms use controlled components for predictable state
decisions.alternative: Uncontrolled (ref-based) has performance benefits but less control

decisions.title: Field-level validation
decisions.rationale: Validate each field independently for better UX
decisions.alternative: Form-level validation is simpler but less responsive

decisions.title: useForm with validators
decisions.rationale: TanStack Form validators run on blur and submit
decisions.alternative: Manual onChange validation is more verbose

changelog.change: Initial form template: 5 field atoms dispatched by FieldType

purpose: Authentication via Better Auth — a modern, TypeScript-first auth framework

decisions.title: Singleton pattern
decisions.rationale: One auth instance exported from lib/auth.ts, reused everywhere
decisions.alternative: Multiple instances can cause session conflicts

decisions.title: Database sessions
decisions.rationale: Sessions stored in SQLite via Drizzle adapter
decisions.alternative: JWT sessions are stateless but harder to revoke

decisions.title: Provider-based
decisions.rationale: GitHub/Google OAuth configured in betterAuth({ socialProviders: {} })
decisions.alternative: Email/password also supported via emailAndPassword plugin

branches.outcome: Pass providers=['github','google'] to tsx add auth
Pass auth=true to tsx generate server-fn

changelog.change: Initial auth: betterAuth config with social providers and session fields
